title: Red's Mana-Crystals
intro: A factor of the Gilded Reach comes to market with a locked case of mana-crystals — charged, tradeable, and priced to the last coin. There are certified stones with a Red seal, and cheaper unsealed ones for a buyer willing to trust his own eye.

advisors.text: Coin for power, Archon — the oldest trade there is. The sealed stones are true. The cheap ones are cheap for a reason.
I can tell a charged crystal from a dead one at ten paces. Let me pick through the unsealed lot — we could rob him blind.

choices.label: Buy certified crystals from the Reach

win.text: The seal is good and the stones are full. Coin changes hands, and Runehold's reserves with it — a clean and costly bargain.
win.saga: You bought Red's certified mana-crystals for a heavy purse, and filled the coven's reserves cleanly.

choices.label: Buy the cheap unsealed lot on your own eye

win.text: Your read is true — the unsealed lot is fuller than the factor knew, and you carry off a bargain that stings his ledger.
win.saga: You judged Red's unsealed mana-crystals by your own lore and carried off a bargain of real charge.

lose.text: Half the lot is dead glass. You paid little, but you got little, and the factor's smile follows you out of the market.
lose.saga: You gambled on Red's cheap mana-crystals and got half a case of dead glass for your coin.

choices.label: Buy nothing — the coven is no one's customer

win.text: You wave the case shut. Runehold will gather its own power, in its own time.
win.saga: You turned away Red's mana-crystals, unwilling to buy what the coven could gather itself.
